claim: Path B microcap basket (8 names) sized at 8% each
what_was_wrong: Surfaced trades despite filter_cascade returning 0 actionable names; macro was RISK_OFF -0.92 with no name above 0.75 floor
correction: Hard rule encoded in generate_pro_brief.py: NO long picks when cascade=0
lesson: Discipline gate is binding. User pressure for action does not override the cascade verdict.

claim: 5 KEEP factors found via IC test (alpha_volume_signed_revert, amihud_20d, rv_60d, vol_of_vol_60d, turnover_skew_20d)
what_was_wrong: IC was strong (≥0.02) but portfolio A/B test showed -0.06pp average lift across 9 years — high IC ≠ portfolio lift when factors correlate with existing inputs
correction: Demoted all 5 to DROP_AB_FAIL in factor_registry.json; updated factor_evaluator.py to require IC_PASSED → portfolio A/B → KEEP
lesson: IC is a screen, not a gate. Gate must be portfolio-level lift via backtest_10yr_with_factors.

claim: Production v3 catalyst+sentiment additions deliver +7.46% mean 7d return
what_was_wrong: +14.6pp lift in 2024 vs -0.3pp in 2025 — unstable across years. Mean was misleading; median tells truth.
correction: Always report median alongside mean; flag instability when years diverge >2x
lesson: Mean returns are fat-tail-corrupted. If 2024 and 2025 diverge by 14pp, the strategy isn't generalising.

what_was_wrong: Empirically falsified by joint_signal_analyzer.py: 3-signal stocks have 40.4% hit rate vs 42.8% baseline. Signals correlate, don't multiply.
correction: Removed stacking thesis; signals identified as non-independent in code
lesson: Stacking only works when signals are independent. Test independence empirically before claiming it.

claim: Multibagger basket: 26 names at 0.86+ → 90% calibrated to double in 180d
what_was_wrong: Calibration was in-sample. User-corrected risk_envelope.py: real prospective hit rate is 12.4% per name (41% basket-level), NOT 90%. Expected ann is -1% (negative EV) without regime gate.
correction: find_180d_frontier_honest.py uses strict prospective protocol (calibrate on 2024 only, test on 2025 only). Real number: max uptick at 80% confidence = 15% in 180d.
lesson: Isotonic on the same OOF being scored is leakage. Must fit calibrator on strictly prior data.

claim: Strategy delivers 30-50% ann (and 262% expected per multibagger basket)
what_was_wrong: 9-year backtest forced top-5 trades EVERY day, including chop days when no name had 0.95+ score → 2018, 2019 negative because of this. With dynamic gate (cash on no-fire days), 9-year median is +8% ann (cash-dominated)
correction: backtest_dynamic_gated.py implements the right test. Honest 9-year median: ~+8% with worst year +7% (cash). Below 30% min target.
lesson: Dynamic > static. The discipline gate transforms outcome distribution. To hit 30%+ unlevered, need market-neutral overlay or wider gate.

claim: Agent prompts in prompts/ directory are routed through every user request
what_was_wrong: Prompts existed as documentation only; not programmatically invoked. Devil's advocate fired AFTER claims, not before.
correction: Built run_agent.py to programmatically invoke any prompt as a Claude API system message. Going forward, material claims must run through devils_advocate first.
lesson: Documented standards are not enforced standards. Must be runtime-wired.

claim: find_high_conviction.py auto-loads scr_*, qvm_*, acad_* prefix features and produces calibrated daily picks
what_was_wrong: 47 of those features (18 Screener + 20+ QVM + 9 Academic) are point-in-time today-snapshots broadcast as constant per-symbol values across all historical training rows. The 2018 training rows for RELIANCE get the same scr_pe (today's), qvm_qvm_rank (today's), and acad_mom_12_1 (computed from 2026 closing prices) as the 2026 rows. Severity: CATASTROPHIC look-ahead. Every claim emitted by find_high_conviction.py since the extras were enabled is structurally inflated. Developer comment in build_academic_alphas.py L194-L195 acknowledges the design: 'academic_alphas only has TODAYS snapshot. We broadcast it forward as a static feature (same caveat as Screener fundamentals).'
correction: 1) All find_high_conviction outputs marked CONTAMINATED in HANDOFF until time-series fundamentals layer ships. 2) Detailed audit at reports/leakage_audit_20260501.md. 3) Remediation: build proper effective_from_date join from data/fundamentals_full_history/ archives; rebuild derived_ratios + academic_alphas with per-date semantics; drop snapshot-broadcast columns until rebuilt.
lesson: Auto-discovery of features by prefix is a leak amplifier. Any feature whose value is constant per symbol across all dates is either a leak or useless. Build a contract: every feature must vary per (symbol, date) OR be explicitly justified as constant.

claim: backtest_dynamic_gated.py and find_180d_frontier_honest.py results are honest
what_was_wrong: Audit verified these two scripts ingest only BASE_FEATS (price/technical) and do NOT touch the leaked screener/qvm/academic columns. So their published numbers (~85% prospective hit @ +15%/180d, +8% 9-year median ann) survive the leakage audit.
correction: No correction needed; honest claims preserved. But note: any future ensemble or stacked variant must declare which feature columns it ingests and prove they pass the (symbol, date) variance check.
lesson: Compartmentalization saved us. The two pipelines that DID stay close to the discipline of HANDOFF turned out to be the only publishable ones. Future model variants should default to BASE_FEATS-only until extra features are independently validated.

claim: stock_daily_facts_adjusted_2015plus.parquet is split/bonus adjusted as the filename suggests
what_was_wrong: Audit triggered by user question 'split etc taken into account hai na?' Found a real adjustment hole. Sample failures: BESTAGRO 1:2 bonus on 2026-01-16 went UNADJUSTED in the price series — close 289.47 → 29.65 (a phantom -89.8% drop) on the ex-date. Multiple gold/silver ETFs (HDFCMFGETF, SETFGOLD, LICMFGOLD, GOLDBEES, etc.) show similar -99% drops on what are clearly unit-restructuring dates. The corporate_actions table itself appears to undercount the actual factor for BESTAGRO (recorded adjustment_factor=1.5 vs observed price dilution ~10x). This means the model's training data contains phantom -90% returns for some symbols, biasing the 9-year base rate of '2x in 180d' UPWARD because some of those 'doubles' were actually bonus-event reversals from a phantom-low close.
correction: 1) Today's live prediction (live_predictions/2026-05-04.json): top-5 picks (KENNAMET, KOVAI, NOVARTIND, AUSOMENT, KIRLFER) audited individually — none have splits/bonuses or suspect price jumps; per-name integrity preserved. The forward outcome measurement is also clean because the broker handles the position-side adjustment automatically. 2) The model's calibrator (8.47% prospective base rate, score_100pct values) has unknown upward bias from contaminated training; absolute probability claims should be treated as strong relative rankings. 3) Remediation: build src/agentic/apply_adjustments.py that uses corporate_actions.parquet to back-adjust the close series symbol-by-symbol with the correct factor, then regenerate stock_daily_facts_adjusted_2015plus.parquet from raw + the new adjustment layer. Also: cross-validate corporate_actions adjustment_factor against the observed price-jump magnitude on each ex_date, flag mismatches. 4) Add CI test: tests/test_no_unadjusted_corporate_actions.py asserts no symbol has a single-day drop sharper than -25% on any date that overlaps a corporate_actions ex_date.
lesson: Filename suffixes lie. _adjusted in a filename is a label, not a contract. Verify by sampling known-event dates (RELIANCE 2017 bonus, INFY 2018 bonus, ADANIPOWER 2025 split) and checking the actual price series. The user's instinct ('split etc taken into account hai na?') was the right rigor question.
